setting.note: A corrupt press baron lies dying in his Mexico City mansion, and his fevered memory ranges back across the making of modern Mexico: his birth on a Veracruz estate, the Revolution in which he fought and betrayed, the marriage into a Puebla landowning family that founded his fortune, and the empire of newspapers and deals he built in the capital. Fuentes maps a life onto a country, the deathbed in the city holding all the places that made Artemio Cruz.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Carlos Fuentes, first published 1962 (La muerte de Artemio Cruz). Mapped as a cited work: no text stored, no passages reproduced. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: The Death of Artemio Cruz, Carlos Fuentes (1962). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author's estate or publisher.

palette.note: The gold of the fortune Artemio Cruz amasses out of a betrayed revolution, the wealth that cannot buy back a single true thing. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
